workflows.title: 🧩subworkflow - aws close security group
workflows.guide:
## 🔐 **Subworkflow: Close Traffic to and from an AWS Security Group**

🚫 This subworkflow removes **all ingress and egress rules** from a specified AWS security group, effectively blocking **all inbound and outbound traffic**.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **awssecuritygroupid** (Required): 🆔 The ID of the AWS security group to modify.  
- **awsregion** (Required): 🌍 The AWS region where the security group is located.  
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 The name of the Dynatrace OIDC connection used to authenticate with AWS.

---

### ⏳ **Execution Behavior**

⏱️ The subworkflow waits for the AWS Systems Manager (SSM) document execution to complete.  
❌ If the SSM document execution fails, this subworkflow will also fail.

---

### 📘 **AWS SSM Document Used**

This subworkflow uses the out-of-the-box AWS Systems Manager document/runbook:  
🔗 https://us-east-1.console.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager/documents/AWS-CloseSecurityGroup/description?region=us-east-1

---

### 🔗 **Dependencies**

This subworkflow depends on the following subworkflow:

- 🧩 [subworkflow - aws wait for systems manager document execution](/ui/apps/dynatrace.automations/workflows/20661ec0-29d5-471f-9973-29679d5fe908)

workflows.title: 🧩subworkflow - aws kill process on ec2 instance (linux)
workflows.guide:
## 🔪 **Subworkflow to Kill a Process on a Linux AWS EC2 Instance**

🖥️ This subworkflow provides an **easy and abstracted way** to **terminate a running process on a Linux EC2 instance**—without requiring any knowledge of how AWS Systems Manager (SSM) works.

It executes the process kill command via AWS SSM, **waits for the command execution to complete**, and **validates whether the execution was successful**.  
❌ If the execution fails, the subworkflow fails as well, allowing the **parent workflow to restart on error**.

---

### ✅ **Requirements**

⚠️ For this subworkflow to run successfully:
- The EC2 instance **must be a Linux instance**
- The EC2 instance **must be managed by AWS Systems Manager (SSM)**

📖 AWS documentation on configuring SSM-managed instances:  
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager/latest/userguide/systems-manager-setting-up-ec2.html

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **awsregion** (Required): 🌍 The AWS region where the EC2 instance resides.  
- **instanceid** (Required): 🆔 The EC2 instance ID on which the process will be terminated.  
- **process_name** (Required): 🧾 The name of the process that should be killed.  
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 The Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection used to authenticate with AWS.

---

### 📘 **AWS SSM Document Used**

This subworkflow uses the out-of-the-box AWS Systems Manager document:

🔗 https://us-east-1.console.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager/documents/AWS-RunShellScript/description?region=us-east-1

---

### 🧠 **Workflow Logic**

- 📤 The subworkflow sends a shell command to the AWS SSM document **`AWS-RunShellScript`**
- 🔪 The specified process is terminated on the Linux EC2 instance
- ⏳ The subworkflow waits for the command execution to complete
- ✅ If successful, the subworkflow completes
- ❌ If execution fails, the subworkflow fails to enable **restart-on-error** handling in the parent workflow

---

This subworkflow Needs subworkflow  
[🧩subworkflow - aws wait for run command execution](/ui/apps/dynatrace.automations/workflows/97218693-adf0-4c82-a295-e43d848b0b4d?trigger=&view=live)

---

workflows.title: 🧩subworkflow - aws davis forecast for aws volume usage
workflows.guide:
## 📈 **Subworkflow for Forecasting AWS Volume Disk Usage**

🔮 This subworkflow provides a streamlined method for **forecasting disk usage of an AWS EBS volume** using Dynatrace Davis AI.

---

### 🕒 **Overview**

Since an AWS volume can only be extended **once every 6 hours**, this subworkflow estimates the **maximum expected disk usage within that time window**.  
📊 The forecasted value can then be passed to a workflow task responsible for extending the volume to the predicted size.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **host_entity_id** (Required): 🖥️ The Dynatrace Host entity ID associated with the AWS volume.

- **forecast_method** (Required): 📐 Specifies which forecast prediction to use.  
  Possible values:
  - **point** → `dt.davis.forecast:point`
  - **upper** → `dt.davis.forecast:upper`
  - **lower** → `dt.davis.forecast:lower`

  📚 More information about these methods:
  - https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/discover-dynatrace/platform/davis-ai/ai-models/forecast-analysis  
  - https://www.dynatrace.com/news/blog/stay-ahead-of-the-game-forecast-it-capacity-with-dynatrace-grail-and-davis-ai/

- **on_forecast_failure_increase_by_percentage_of_total_diskspace** (Required): 🛟  
  A fallback percentage of the total volume size used to calculate an extension size if:
  - forecasting fails due to insufficient data, or  
  - the forecasted value is smaller than the current volume size.

---

### 🔗 **Workflow Requirement**

Workflow needs subworkflow  
[subworkflow - aws get host data of ec2 instance](/ui/apps/dynatrace.automations/workflows/3e5454a4-50ad-4dab-914c-638b6df5b283?trigger=&view=live)

---

### 🧠 **Workflow Logic**

- ▶️ When triggered, the subworkflow uses the **host_entity_id** to forecast disk usage **6 hours into the future**.
- ⚠️ If the forecast fails due to insufficient data **or** the predicted usage is less than the current volume size, the subworkflow performs an alternative calculation using the fallback parameter.

---

### 🔁 **Alternative Calculation**

If forecasting is unsuccessful, the volume is extended using the following formula:

workflows.guideSummary: 📈 Subworkflow for Forecasting AWS Volume Disk Usage
workflows.description:
Subworkflow: Forecasting AWS Volume Disk Usage
This subworkflow provides a streamlined method for forecasting the disk usage of an AWS EBS volume.

Since an AWS volume can only be extended once every 6 hours, this subworkflow estimates the maximum expected usage within that time window.
The forecasted value can then be passed to a workflow task responsible for extending the volume to the predicted size.

Parameters
host_entity_id (Required):
The Dynatrace Host entity ID associated with the AWS volume.

forecast_method (Required):
possible values are "point", "upper", "lower" depending on which of the prediction outcomes you would like to use
point = dt.davis.forecast:point
upper = dt.davis.forecast:upper
lower = dt.davis.forecast:lower
You can find more information about these methods in the documentation:
https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/discover-dynatrace/platform/davis-ai/ai-models/forecast-analysis
https://www.dynatrace.com/news/blog/stay-ahead-of-the-game-forecast-it-capacity-with-dynatrace-grail-and-davis-ai/

on_forecast_failure_increase_by_percentage_of_total_diskspace (Required):
A fallback percentage of the total volume size used to calculate an extension size if the forecast fails or returns a value smaller than the current volume size.

Workflow Logic
When triggered, the subworkflow uses the host_entity_id to forecast the disk usage 6 hours into the future.
If the forecast failsdue to insufficient data or if the predicted usage is less than the current volume sizethe subworkflow performs an alternative calculation using the fallback parameter.

Alternative Calculation
If forecasting is unsuccessful, the volume is extended using the following formula:
New Volume Size = Current Volume Size + (Current Volume Size  Fallback Percentage)

For example:
If the current volume size is 100 GB and the fallback percentage is 50%, the new volume size will be:
100 GB + (100 GB  0.50) = 150 GB

workflows.title: 🧩subworkflow - aws delete cloudformation stack
workflows.guide:
## 🗑️ **Subworkflow to Delete an AWS CloudFormation Stack**

🧹 This subworkflow provides a simple and reliable way to **trigger the deletion of an AWS CloudFormation stack** using AWS Systems Manager (SSM).

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **cf_stackname** (Required): 🏷️ The name of the CloudFormation stack to delete.  
- **awsregion** (Required): 🌍 The AWS region in which the CloudFormation stack will be deleted.  
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 The name of the Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection used for authentication.

---

### 📘 **Prerequisites**

✅ This subworkflow requires the custom AWS Systems Manager (SSM) document  
**`Dynwf-DeleteCloudFormationStack`** to be available in the AWS region provided by the `awsregion` parameter.

---

### 🧠 **Workflow Logic**

- 📤 The subworkflow sends a command to delete the CloudFormation stack to the  
  AWS SSM document **`Dynwf-DeleteCloudFormationStack`**
- ⏳ It waits for the SSM document execution to complete
- ✅ The result is checked to verify that the deletion was successful
- ❌ If the deletion fails, the subworkflow fails to enable **restart-on-error** behavior in the parent workflow

---

### 🔗 **Workflow Requirement**

Workflow needs subworkflow  
[subworkflow - aws wait for systems manager document execution](/ui/apps/dynatrace.automations/workflows/20661ec0-29d5-471f-9973-29679d5fe908)

---

## 🛠️ **Steps to Import AWS SSM Document / Runbook YAML**

1. 🔗 Open the following link:  
   https://us-east-1.console.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager/documents/create-document?region=us-east-1#documentType=Automation  
   *(You must be logged in to the AWS Management Console.)*

2. 🌍 If needed, change the AWS region in the console.

3. ✏️ In the upper-left corner, change the runbook name to:  
   **`Dynwf-DeleteCloudFormationStack`**

4. 🧾 Click the **`{}` Code** button next to the runbook name to open the YAML definition.

5. 📋 Paste the following YAML content into the code editor:

```yaml
schemaVersion: '0.3'

parameters:
  stackname:
    type: String

mainSteps:
  - name: DeleteCloudFormationStack
    action: aws:deleteStack
    isEnd: true
    inputs:
      StackName: '{{ stackname }}'

workflows.guide:
## 🧹 **Subworkflow to Delete an Unused Non-Default AWS Security Group**

🔐 This subworkflow deletes a **non-default AWS security group** that is **not associated with any elastic network interface (ENI)**.

A **non-default security group** is defined as any security group whose name is **not** `default`.  
⚠️ If the security group ID passed to this subworkflow belongs to a **default security group**, **no changes will be made**.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **awssecuritygroupid** (Required): 🆔 The AWS security group ID to delete.  
- **assumerolearn** (Required): 🎭 The AWS IAM role ARN to assume, which must allow deletion of security groups.  
- **awsregion** (Required): 🌍 The AWS region where the security group resides.  
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 The name of the Dynatrace OIDC connection used to authenticate with AWS.

---

### ⏳ **Execution Behavior**

⏱️ The subworkflow waits for the AWS Systems Manager (SSM) document execution to complete.  
❌ If the SSM document execution fails, this subworkflow will also fail.

---

### 📘 **AWS SSM Document Used**

This subworkflow uses the out-of-the-box AWS Systems Manager document:

🔗 https://us-east-1.console.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager/documents/AWSConfigRemediation-DeleteUnusedSecurityGroup/description?region=us-east-1

---

### 🧠 **Workflow Logic**

- 🔍 The subworkflow validates that the specified security group:
  - is **not** a default security group, and  
  - is **not attached** to any elastic network interface (ENI)
- 📤 A delete command is sent to the AWS SSM document  
  **`AWSConfigRemediation-DeleteUnusedSecurityGroup`**
- ⏳ The subworkflow waits for execution to complete
- ✅ If the deletion is successful, the workflow completes
- ❌ If the deletion fails, the subworkflow fails to allow **restart-on-error** handling in the parent workflow

---
``

workflows.title: 🧩subworkflow - aws disable public access for security group
workflows.guide:
## 🚫 **Subworkflow to Disable SSH and RDP Public Access on an AWS Security Group**

🔐 This subworkflow disables **SSH (22)** and **RDP (3389)** ingress rules that are:
- opened to a **specific IP address**, or  
- opened to **all addresses (`0.0.0.0/0`)** if no IP address is specified.

⚠️ *The security group must already have existing ingress rules on the SSH and/or RDP ports for traffic to be disabled.*

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **awssecuritygroupid** (Required): 🆔 The AWS security group ID for which traffic should be restricted.  
- **ipaddresstoblock** (Optional): 🌐 An IPv4 or IPv6 CIDR block to block access from  
  *(example: `1.2.3.4/32`)*.  
  If not provided, **all IP addresses** (including `0.0.0.0/0`) are considered.
- **awsregion** (Required): 🌍 The AWS region where the security group resides.  
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 The name of the Dynatrace OIDC connection used to authenticate with AWS.

---

### ⏳ **Execution Behavior**

⏱️ This subworkflow waits for the AWS Systems Manager (SSM) document execution to complete.  
❌ If the SSM document execution fails, this subworkflow will also fail.

---

### 📘 **AWS SSM Document Used**

This subworkflow uses the out-of-the-box AWS Systems Manager document:

🔗 **AWS-DisablePublicAccessForSecurityGroup**

---

### 🧠 **Workflow Logic**

- 🔍 The subworkflow evaluates existing ingress rules on the specified security group
- 🚫 SSH (22) and RDP (3389) rules matching the specified IP address (or all addresses) are disabled
- 📤 The remediation is executed via the AWS SSM document
- ⏳ The subworkflow waits for execution to complete
- ✅ If successful, the workflow completes
- ❌ If execution fails, the subworkflow fails to allow **restart-on-error** handling in the parent workflow

---

### 🔗 **Workflow Requirement**

This subworkflow Needs subworkflow  
[subworkflow - aws wait for systems manager document execution](/ui/apps/dynatrace.automations/workflows/20661ec0-29d5-471f-9973-29679d5fe908)

workflows.title: 🧩subworkflow - aws extend ebs volume
workflows.guide:
## 📦 **Subworkflow to Extend AWS Volumes (Once Every 6 Hours)**

📈 This subworkflow provides a simple and reliable way to **extend AWS EBS volumes** attached to an EC2 instance.  
⏱️ Since AWS volumes can only be extended **once every 6 hours**, this workflow is designed to safely handle volume and file system resizing.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **osType** (Required): 🖥️ The operating system type of the EC2 instance  
  *(Supported values: `Windows` or `Linux`)*

- **SizeGib** (Required): 📏 The target size to extend the volume to (in **GiB**).

- **VolumeId** (Required): 🆔 The ID of the EBS volume to be extended.

- **awsregion** (Required): 🌍 The AWS region where the volume resides.

- **InstanceId** (Required): 🧾 The EC2 instance ID that the volume is attached to.

- **MountPoint** (Optional): 📂 The mount point of the partition to be extended  
  *(Required for Linux, e.g. `/`, `/data`)*

- **DriveLetter** (Optional): 💽 The drive letter to be extended  
  *(Required for Windows, e.g. `C:`)*

- **keepSnapShot** (Optional): 📸 Boolean flag indicating whether to retain the snapshot after a successful resize  
  *(Defaults to `true`)*

- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 The Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection used for authentication.

- **AutomationAssumeRolearn** (Optional): 🎭 The ARN of the IAM role that allows Automation to perform actions on your behalf.

---

### ✅ **Requirements**

⚠️ This subworkflow **only works on SSM-managed AWS EC2 instances**.

📖 Refer to the AWS documentation below to ensure your EC2 instances are managed by AWS Systems Manager:  
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager/latest/userguide/systems-manager-setting-up-ec2.html

---

### 📘 **AWS SSM Documents Used**

This subworkflow requires the following **out-of-the-box AWS Systems Manager document**:

- **`aws-extendebsvolume`**  
  🔗 https://docs.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager-automation-runbooks/latest/userguide/aws-extendebsvolume.html

---

### 🧠 **Provided Functionality**

The **`aws-extendebsvolume`** SSM document performs the following actions:

- 📸 **Create a snapshot** of the volume *(optional)*  
- 📦 **Resize the EBS volume** at the AWS level  
- 🧩 **Resize partitions and file systems** on the operating system (Linux or Windows)

---

workflows.guide:
## 🔄 **Subworkflow to Reboot an AWS EC2 Instance**

♻️ This subworkflow provides a simple and reliable way to **reboot an AWS EC2 instance** and **wait until the instance is back online** before continuing.

It is commonly used as a remediation or recovery step in automation workflows where a controlled restart is required.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **instanceIds** (Required): 🆔 The EC2 instance ID to reboot.  
- **awsRegion** (Required): 🌍 The AWS region in which the EC2 instance resides.  
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 The name of the Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection used to authenticate with AWS.

---

### ⏳ **Execution Behavior**

⏱️ The subworkflow triggers the EC2 reboot operation and **waits until the instance is fully back up and reachable**.  
❌ If the reboot operation fails, the subworkflow will fail to allow **restart-on-error** handling in the parent workflow.

---

### 🧠 **Usage Context**

- ✅ Commonly used for **self-healing and remediation workflows**
- ✅ Ensures downstream steps only execute once the instance is available again
- ✅ Suitable for automation triggered by problems, events, or maintenance workflows
- ✅ Integrates securely using the **Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection**

---

workflows.guide:
## 🔧 **Subworkflow to Resize an AWS EC2 Instance (Change Instance Type)**

📐 This subworkflow provides a simple and controlled way to **resize an AWS EC2 instance by changing its instance type** (for example, scaling from `t3.small` to `t3.medium`).

It is commonly used for **cost optimization, performance tuning, or remediation workflows** where instance capacity needs to be adjusted dynamically.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **resize_to_instance_type** (Required): 🏷️ The target EC2 instance type  
  *(e.g. `t3.small`, `t3.medium`, `m5.large`)*

- **instanceid** (Required): 🆔 The ID of the EC2 instance to resize.

- **awsregion** (Required): 🌍 The AWS region where the EC2 instance resides.

- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 The name of the Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection used to authenticate with AWS.

---

### 📘 **AWS SSM Document Used**

This subworkflow uses the out-of-the-box AWS Systems Manager document:

🔗 https://us-east-1.console.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager/documents/AWS-ResizeInstance/description?region=us-east-1

---

### ⏳ **Execution Behavior**

⏱️ The subworkflow waits for the AWS Systems Manager (SSM) document execution to complete.  
❌ If the SSM document execution fails, this subworkflow will also fail.

---

### 🧠 **Workflow Logic**

- 📤 The subworkflow sends a resize command to the AWS SSM document

## ▶️ **Subworkflow to Run a Command on an AWS EC2 Instance**

🖥️ This subworkflow provides an **easy and abstracted way** to execute a command on an **AWS EC2 instance** without requiring any knowledge of how AWS Systems Manager (SSM) works.

It triggers the execution of a command via AWS SSM, **waits until the command execution has completed**, and **validates whether the execution was successful**.  
❌ If the execution fails, the subworkflow will fail so the **parent workflow can restart on error**.

---

### ✅ **Requirements**

⚠️ In order for this subworkflow to execute successfully, the EC2 instance **must be a Systems Manager (SSM) managed instance**.

📖 AWS documentation:  
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager/latest/userguide/systems-manager-setting-up-ec2.html

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **commands** (Required): 🧾 The command or script to run on the EC2 instance.  
- **awsregion** (Required): 🌍 The AWS region where the EC2 instance resides.  
- **instanceid** (Required): 🆔 The EC2 instance ID on which the command will run.  
- **executionTimeout** (Required): ⏱️ The execution timeout for the command.  
- **workingDirectory** (Required): 📂 The working directory in which the command is executed.  
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 The Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection used to authenticate with AWS.

---

### 📘 **AWS SSM Document Used**

This subworkflow uses the out-of-the-box AWS Systems Manager document:

🔗 https://us-east-1.console.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager/documents/AWS-RunShellScript/description?region=us-east-1

---

### 🧠 **Workflow Logic**

- 📤 The subworkflow sends the command to the AWS SSM document **`AWS-RunShellScript`**
- ⏳ It waits for the command execution to complete
- ✅ The execution result is checked for success
- ❌ If execution fails, the subworkflow fails to allow **restart-on-error** handling in the parent workflow

---

🧩[subworkflow - aws wait for run command execution](/ui/apps/dynatrace.automations/workflows/97218693-adf0-4c82-a295-e43d848b0b4d)

workflows.title: 🧩subworkflow - aws s3 block public access for bucket
workflows.guide:
## 🛡️ **Subworkflow to Block Public Access to an Amazon S3 Bucket**

🔒 This subworkflow provides a simple and standardized way to **block public access to an Amazon S3 bucket** by configuring S3 public access block settings.

It is commonly used as part of **security remediation workflows** to prevent unintended public exposure of S3 buckets.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **awsregion** (Required): 🌍 The AWS region where the S3 bucket resides.  
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 The name of the Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection used to authenticate with AWS.  
- **BucketName** (Required): 🪣 The name of the S3 bucket *(not the ARN)*.

- **RestrictPublicBuckets** (Optional): 🚫  
  Specifies whether Amazon S3 should restrict public bucket policies.  
  When set to `true`, access is limited to AWS services and authorized users within the same account if the bucket has a public policy.

- **BlockPublicAcls** (Optional): 📵  
  Specifies whether Amazon S3 should block public access control lists (ACLs) for the bucket and its objects.

- **IgnorePublicAcls** (Optional): 🙈  
  Specifies whether Amazon S3 should ignore all public ACLs on the bucket and its objects.  
  When set to `true`, any existing public ACLs are ignored.

- **BlockPublicPolicy** (Optional): 🧱  
  Specifies whether Amazon S3 should block public bucket policies.  
  When set to `true`, Amazon S3 rejects calls to `PUT Bucket policy` if the policy allows public access.

- **AutomationAssumeRole** (Required): 🎭  
  The ARN of the IAM role that allows Automation to perform actions on your behalf.

- **StabilizationPeriod** (Optional): ⏱️  
  The duration (in seconds) to wait for remediation stabilization.  
  Must be between **1** and **604,799** seconds.

---

### 🧠 **Workflow Logic**

- 🔍 The subworkflow evaluates the current public access configuration of the specified S3 bucket
- 🛡️ Public access settings are applied based on the provided parameters
- ✅ If successful, the bucket is protected against unintended public exposure
- ❌ If execution fails, the subworkflow fails to allow **restart-on-error** handling in the parent workflow

---

workflows.title: 🧩subworkflow - aws scan for security groups with ports open to 0.0.0.0/0
workflows.guide:
## 🔍 **Subworkflow to Scan for Publicly Exposed AWS Security Group Ingress Rules**

🚨 This subworkflow scans AWS security groups for **ingress rule definitions with ports open to `0.0.0.0/0`**, exposing them to **the entire internet**, and **raises a custom Dynatrace alert** when such configurations are detected.

---

### ⏱️ **Why This Matters**

AWS Security Hub typically scans for these misconfigurations only every **12–24 hours**.  
⚡ This subworkflow provides a **more frequent and proactive alternative**, allowing you to detect and respond to insecure security group configurations much faster.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 The name of the Dynatrace OIDC connection used to authenticate with AWS.  
- **regions_to_search** (Required): 🌍 One or more AWS regions to scan for security groups with ingress rules open to `0.0.0.0/0`.

---

### 🧠 **Workflow Logic**

- 🔍 The subworkflow scans security groups across the specified AWS regions
- 🚫 Ingress rules allowing access from `0.0.0.0/0` are identified
- 🚨 A **custom Dynatrace alert** is raised when insecure configurations are detected
- ✅ Enables downstream workflows to remediate or notify based on the alert

---

workflows.guide:
## ⏳ **Subworkflow to Wait for AWS SSM Run Command Execution to Finish**

🧠 This subworkflow is used by the **“aws run command on EC2 instance”** subworkflow to **wait for an AWS SSM Run Command execution to complete** and **validate the execution status**.

✅ If the command execution is successful, the subworkflow completes.  
❌ If the execution fails, the subworkflow fails as well—allowing **parent workflows to react with restart-on-error or alternative handling**.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **awsregion** (Required): 🌍 The AWS region in which the SSM Run Command is executed.  
- **awsinstanceid** (Required): 🆔 The EC2 instance ID on which the Run Command was executed.  
- **CommandId** (Required): 🧾 The command ID of the Run Command execution  
  *(returned by the AWS Run Command action)*.  
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 The Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection used to authenticate with AWS.

---

### 🧠 **Workflow Logic**

- ⏳ The subworkflow continuously checks the status of the specified Run Command execution
- ✅ If the execution completes successfully, the subworkflow finishes
- ❌ If the execution fails or returns an error state, the subworkflow fails
- 🔁 This enables **restart-on-error** or compensating logic in the parent workflow

---

### 🧩 **Usage Context**

- ✅ Designed as a **helper / control-flow subworkflow**
- ✅ Ensures downstream workflow steps only execute after command completion
- ✅ Commonly paired with:
  - Run command on EC2 instance
  - Remediation scripts
  - Validation and recovery workflows

---

## ⏳ **Subworkflow to Wait for AWS SSM Document Execution to Finish**

🧠 This subworkflow is used by other subworkflows that **execute AWS SSM documents** to **wait for the execution to complete** and **validate the execution status**.

✅ If the SSM document execution completes successfully, the subworkflow finishes.  
❌ If the execution fails, the subworkflow fails as well—allowing **parent workflows to respond with rerun-on-error or alternative handling logic**.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **awsregion** (Required): 🌍 The AWS region in which the SSM document is executed.  
- **AutomationExecutionId** (Required): 🧾 The execution ID of the SSM document  
  *(returned by the Start SSM Automation action)*.  
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 The Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection used to authenticate with AWS.

workflows.guide:
## 🗑️ **Subworkflow to Delete All Workflows of a Specific Owner**

⚠️ This subworkflow provides a powerful way to **delete all workflows owned by a specific user**.

When the subworkflow starts, it:
- 👤 Determines the **owner of the running workflow**
- 🔍 Searches for **all workflows owned by that user**
- 🗑️ **Deletes every workflow it finds**

---

### 🚨 **Important Warning**

⚠️ **This action is destructive and irreversible.**

All workflows owned by the identified user will be **permanently deleted**.

✅ **Ensure workflows are backed up or exported before running this subworkflow.**

This subworkflow should only be used in **controlled governance, cleanup, or emergency scenarios**.

---

### 🎯 **Use Case**

Typical use cases include:

- 🚫 Removing **unauthorized or non-compliant workflows**
- 🧼 Performing **bulk cleanup** during governance enforcement
- 🛡️ Responding to security or audit findings
- 🔁 Resetting environments during testing or enablement activities

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

🚫 This subworkflow **does not require any input parameters**.

---

### 🧠 **Workflow Logic**

- 👤 Identify the owner of the executing workflow
- 🔍 Locate all workflows owned by that user
- 🗑️ Delete each workflow
- ✅ Complete if successful
- ❌ Fail if an error occurs, allowing the **parent workflow to respond appropriately**

---

### 🧩 **Usage Context**

- ✅ Designed for **workflow governance and lifecycle enforcement**
- ✅ Intended for **advanced administrative automation**
- ✅ Complements workflow create, export, and validation subworkflows
- ✅ Should be protected by approvals or access controls

workflows.title: 🧩subworkflow - dynatrace check if problem is solved or closed
workflows.guide:
## ✅ **Subworkflow to Check if a Problem Is Solved or Closed**

🧠 This subworkflow is used to **validate whether a specific remediation step has resolved a problem**.

It is especially useful in workflows that execute **multiple remediation actions**, where you want to verify after each step whether the problem has been **solved or closed** before proceeding further.

✅ If the problem is solved or closed, the subworkflow completes successfully.  
❌ If the problem is still open, the subworkflow fails—allowing the **parent workflow to retry or take alternative action**.

---

### 🎯 **Use Case**

When executing iterative remediation steps, this subworkflow enables **automated decision-making** based on the current state of a problem.

You can configure **retry-on-error** behavior in the parent workflow to:
- 🔁 Retry this subworkflow for a defined duration (for example, every minute for 10 minutes)
- ⏳ Periodically re-check whether the problem has been resolved

If the problem is **not resolved within the defined time window**, you can:

- ➕ Trigger an additional remediation step  
- 👤 Escalate the issue to a human operator  

This approach supports **automated, iterative problem resolution** while maintaining full control over escalation and fallback strategies.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **problem_display_id** (Required): 🆔 The problem display ID  
  *(for example: `P-25051158`)*

---

### 🧩 **Usage Context**

- ✅ Designed as a **control and validation subworkflow**
- ✅ Commonly used between remediation steps
- ✅ Enables retry-based logic instead of hard-coded delays
- ✅ Works seamlessly with **restart-on-error** patterns in parent workflows

---

workflows.title: 🧩subworkflow - dynatrace export one or all workflows to existing github repo
workflows.guide:
## 📤 **Subworkflow to Export One or More Workflows to a GitHub Repository**

📦 This subworkflow provides an automated way to **export one or all workflows to a GitHub repository**.

It supports **workflow-as-code** practices by enabling version control, backup, governance, and promotion of workflows across environments.

---

### 🎯 **Use Case**

Typical use cases include:

- 🗂️ Backing up workflows to GitHub for safekeeping
- 🔁 Promoting workflows between environments (dev → test → prod)
- 🛡️ Supporting governance, audit, and compliance requirements
- 📦 Managing workflows as code using Git-based processes

This subworkflow can export:
- ✅ A **single workflow**, or  
- ✅ **all workflows**, depending on configuration and permissions

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **workflowId** (Optional): 🆔 The ID of a specific workflow to export  
  *(for example: `15508756-7ee1-4c03-ad1b-d3d95aca478f`)*  
  If left empty (`""`), **all workflows** will be exported.

- **searchLimit** (Required): 🔢 The maximum number of workflows to export.

- **githubRepoName** (Required): 📁 The name of the **existing GitHub repository** to export workflows to.

- **githubOwnerName** (Required): 👤 The GitHub **owner or organization name**.

- **githubRepoBranchName** (Required): 🌿 The GitHub **branch name** to export to.

- **dynatraceGithubConnection** (Required): 🔑 The Dynatrace GitHub connection name used for authentication.

- **adminAccess** (Required): 🛡️ Boolean flag (`true` / `false`) controlling export scope:
  - `false`: Only workflows the user has permission to access will be exported  
  - `true`: If the user has **workflow-admin** rights, **all workflows** will be exported

---

### 🔗 **GitHub Connection Setup**

Before using this subworkflow, ensure that a **GitHub Connector** is configured in Dynatrace.

📖 Official Dynatrace documentation:  
https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/analyze-explore-automate/workflows/actions/github/github-workflows-setup [1](https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/analyze-explore-automate/workflows/actions/github/github-workflows-setup)

This guide explains how to:
- Configure outbound access to GitHub
- Create and authorize a GitHub connection
- Generate and scope a GitHub personal access token
- Make the connection available to workflows

---

### 🧠 **Workflow Logic**

- 🔍 Identify workflows based on `workflowId`, permissions, and `adminAccess`
- 📤 Export workflow definitions to the specified GitHub repository and branch
- ✅ Complete if export succeeds
- ❌ Fail if export encounters an error, allowing the **parent workflow to retry or react**

---

### 🧩 **Usage Context**

- ✅ Designed for **workflow lifecycle and governance automation**
- ✅ Enables Git-based workflow management
- ✅ Complements workflow create, delete, and validation subworkflows
- ✅ Supports automation-at-scale and CI/CD patterns

---

workflows.title: 🧩subworkflow - dynatrace get all problem data
workflows.guide:
## 🧠 **Subworkflow to Get All Data of a Problem**

🔎 This subworkflow provides an easy way to **retrieve complete problem details** that are **not included in the standard problem trigger**.

Important information—such as **impact**, **affected users**, and other contextual fields—is often required to correctly determine the **priority of a problem** and to synchronize that priority with external tracking systems like **Jira** or **ServiceNow**.

This subworkflow makes it simple to access that additional data **without requiring JavaScript knowledge**.

---

### 🎯 **Use Case**

A common use case is **enriching problem data before creating or updating tickets** in tracking tools.

In addition, this subworkflow includes logic to **set the priority of a Jira issue** based on the data retrieved from the problem card.

⚙️ This logic may vary between environments, but it can be easily adjusted in the  
`// calculate jira impact level` section of the **get‑problem‑info** task.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **event.id** (Required): 🆔 The Dynatrace event ID associated with the problem.

---

### 🧩 **Usage Context**

- ✅ Designed as a **data‑enrichment subworkflow**
- ✅ Provides access to full problem context beyond trigger payloads
- ✅ Enables accurate prioritization in Jira, ServiceNow, and other tools
- ✅ Commonly used early in remediation or escalation workflows

---

workflows.title: 🧩subworkflow - dynatrace ingest custom alert
workflows.guide:
## 🚨 **Subworkflow to Ingest / Create a Custom Alert**

🧩 This subworkflow provides a simple and standardized way to **ingest or create a custom alert** using a JSON definition—**without requiring JavaScript knowledge**.

It is commonly used to **raise custom alerts from automation workflows**, enabling visibility, correlation, and downstream actions based on conditions detected outside of built‑in alerting.

---

### 🎯 **Use Case**

Typical use cases include:

- 🚦 Raising custom alerts from automation logic or validations  
- 🔗 Integrating external signals or conditions into the alerting pipeline  
- 🧠 Driving conditional workflows based on custom alert states  
- 📣 Providing visibility for non-native or derived conditions  

This approach allows teams to extend alerting in a **controlled and repeatable** way.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **Custom Alert Json** (Required): 🧾 The full JSON definition of the custom alert to be ingested or created.

---

### 🧩 **Usage Context**

- ✅ Designed as a **helper / ingestion subworkflow**
- ✅ Eliminates the need to write JavaScript for custom alert creation
- ✅ Enables alert-driven automation patterns
- ✅ Works well with remediation, escalation, and notification workflows

---

workflows.title: 🧩subworkflow - OpenAi Text and Prompting
workflows.guide:
## 🧠 **Subworkflow to Text and Prompt OpenAI**

✍️ This subworkflow provides a simple and automated way to **send text prompts to OpenAI** and use the generated output directly within a workflow.

It is commonly used for **communication, content generation, and response automation** based on operational events or workflow context.

---

### 🎯 **Use Case**

A typical use case is to **generate a business letter or customer communication** for a group of users who were affected by detected problems.

This enables:
- 📣 Automated customer or stakeholder communication  
- 🧾 Consistent, professional messaging  
- ⚡ Faster response without manual drafting  

---

### 🔐 **Authentication Requirement**

⚠️ To use this subworkflow, you must create a **Credential Vault entry** containing your **OpenAI API token**.

The credential must then be referenced in the **`ask-open-ai`** task under the **authentication** settings.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **input** (Required): 🧾 The prompt text to send to OpenAI.  
- **model** (Required): 🧠 The AI model to use  
  *(for example: `gpt-4.1`)*

---

### 🧩 **Usage Context**

- ✅ Designed for **AI-assisted automation**
- ✅ Enables natural-language output from workflows
- ✅ Useful for notifications, summaries, and business communications
- ✅ Can be combined with problem, event, or data-enrichment subworkflows

---

workflows.title: 🧩subworkflow - dynatrace set all workflows of owner to draft
workflows.guide:
## 🧩 **Subworkflow to Set All Workflows of the Owner to Draft**

🛠️ This subworkflow provides an automated way to **set all workflows owned by the current workflow owner to draft**.

When the subworkflow is started, it:
- 👤 Identifies the **owner of the running workflow**
- 🔍 Searches for **all workflows owned by that user**
- 📝 Transitions each workflow into **draft state**

This is especially useful for **governance, bulk control, or emergency rollback scenarios**.

---

### 🎯 **Use Case**

Typical use cases include:

- 🚫 Temporarily disabling all workflows owned by a specific user
- 🧼 Bulk workflow control during governance or audit activities
- 🛡️ Preventing execution of workflows during incidents or investigations
- 🔁 Pausing automation before making bulk changes or cleanups

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

🚫 This subworkflow **does not require any input parameters**.

---

### 🧠 **Workflow Logic**

- 👤 Determine the owner of the executing workflow
- 🔍 Locate all workflows owned by that user
- 📝 Set each workflow to **draft**
- ✅ Complete if successful
- ❌ Fail if an error occurs, allowing the **parent workflow to retry or take alternative action**

---

### 🧩 **Usage Context**

- ✅ Designed for **workflow governance and lifecycle control**
- ✅ Enables centralized bulk actions
- ✅ Complements workflow create, delete, and export subworkflows
- ✅ Supports automation-at-scale best practices

---

workflows.title: 🧩subworkflow - dynatrace set all workflows of owner to live (deployed)
workflows.guide:
## 🚀 **Subworkflow to Set All Workflows of the Owner to Live (Deployed)**

✅ This subworkflow provides an automated way to **deploy all workflows owned by the current workflow owner to Live**.

When the subworkflow is started, it:
- 👤 Identifies the **owner of the running workflow**
- 🔍 Searches for **all workflows owned by that user**
- 🚀 Deploys each workflow to **Live (Deployed)** state

This is especially useful for **bulk activation, controlled rollouts, or recovery scenarios** after maintenance or governance actions.

---

### 🎯 **Use Case**

Typical use cases include:

- ▶️ Re‑enabling all workflows owned by a user after maintenance
- 🔁 Bulk deployment following workflow updates or imports
- 🧪 Promoting workflows from draft to live in enablement or test scenarios
- ⚡ Rapid recovery after workflows were temporarily set to draft

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

🚫 This subworkflow **does not require any input parameters**.

---

### 🧠 **Workflow Logic**

- 👤 Determine the owner of the executing workflow
- 🔍 Locate all workflows owned by that user
- 🚀 Set each workflow to **Live (Deployed)**
- ✅ Complete if successful
- ❌ Fail if an error occurs, allowing the **parent workflow to retry or take alternative action**

---

### 🧩 **Usage Context**

- ✅ Designed for **workflow lifecycle and governance control**
- ✅ Enables centralized bulk deployment actions
- ✅ Complements:
  - Set workflows to draft  
  - Create / delete workflows  
  - Export and import workflows  
- ✅ Supports automation-at-scale and controlled rollout strategies

---
``
